programs.title: Stoking the Fire of Hope
programs.desc: In recent years, night high schools in Japan are admitting more students with international roots. We look at a school in Tokyo and a teacher dedicated to guiding his students toward a better future.

programs.title: Document 72 Hours
programs.desc: . Locals have slurped down bowls of noodles at a 24-hour ramen restaurant in Fukuoka for five decades. For three days, we asked customers why this simple ramen is more than just a cheap meal. Masako Nishijima.

programs.title: Nhk Newsline
programs.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programs.title: My Street Piano
programs.desc: earthquake. A piano is placed in a shopping arcade for a limited period, and people stop to play from their heart.

programs.title: Science View
programs.desc: . Squid and cuttlefish use camouflage not just to hide but to communicate. Vanguard Researchers explores the mechanism behind plant immunity. Tomoko Tashiro, Naoko Yamazaki, Michelle Yamamoto, Yoichi Sato, Tomoko Tina Kimura, Rena Yamada, Ross Mihara, Rachel Walzer.
